Metadata.title: CiteOps — AEO & GEO audits for the answer-engine era
Metadata.description: Lighthouse-inspired CLI that scores web content for Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) and Generative Engine Optimization (GEO). HTML, JSON, and CSV reports plus CI gates.

Hero.title: Measure how well your content works for AI answers not just blue links.
Hero.subtitle: CiteOps audits URLs and local pages for AEO and GEO, explains what failed, and ships reports your team can act on. One install, your pipeline stays yours.

Why.title: Why this matters now
Why.lead: Discovery is splitting: people still search, but they also ask chat assistants that synthesize answers from many sources. If your content is not structured for citation and clarity, you can rank—and still be invisible where decisions happen.
Why.p1: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) and Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) are the practices that make your pages trustworthy, quotable, and machine-legible when models summarize the web.
Why.p2: CiteOps gives you a repeatable score and a punch list—like Lighthouse did for performance—so editors, SEO, and engineering share one definition of “good enough” before you ship.
Why.quote: If you cannot measure it against a checklist, you cannot improve it on every release.

Education.title: AEO & GEO in plain language
Education.subtitle: Both aim for visibility when answers are generated—not just ten blue links. CiteOps checks your content with that lens.
Education.aeoBody: Optimizing for engines that extract direct answers: featured snippets, voice, and structured Q&A surfaces. Strong headings, factual blocks, and clear entity signals help your page become the answer source—not a footnote.
Education.aeoProduct: CiteOps surfaces gaps that weaken answer readiness: clarity, structure, and signals models use when choosing what to quote.
Education.geoBody: Optimizing for large language models that synthesize across sources. Consistent naming, attributable facts, and crawl-friendly markup reduce hallucination risk and improve the odds your brand is represented accurately.
Education.geoProduct: CiteOps scores GEO-oriented signals alongside AEO so you see composite risk, not a single vanity metric.

How.title: How CiteOps works
How.subtitle: From a URL or file to a report you can share in CI or a browser.
How.step1Body: Point the CLI at a URL, a Markdown or HTML file, a folder, or a sitemap. Rate limits and robots.txt are respected unless you override for your own property.
How.step2Body: Checks run against your content; failures include explanations so writers and devs know what to fix.
How.step3Body: Composite, AEO, and GEO scores summarize readiness. Tune with optional .citeops.json when you need project defaults.
How.step4Body: Emit HTML for humans, JSON for automation, or CSV for batches. Use --ci and --threshold to fail builds when scores drop.

Product.title: Built for product teams
Product.subtitle: Everything in the CLI is designed for shipping workflows—not one-off spreadsheets.
Product.card1Body: HTML for stakeholders, JSON for pipelines, CSV for sitemap or folder runs.
Product.card2Body: Optional .citeops.json in the repo or home directory; override path per run.
Product.card3Body: Exit codes for pass, score failure, network errors, and bad input—mirror how you run Lighthouse in CI.
Product.card4Body: Read-only fetches, configurable rate limits, and robots awareness for compliant crawling.
Product.card5Body: llm-citeops overview surfaces capabilities, outputs, and quick-start hints in the terminal.
Product.card6Body: Audit directories of Markdown/HTML or expand sitemaps to CSV for wide coverage.

Action.title: See it in your terminal
Action.subtitle: The overview command summarizes what the tool can do—similar spirit to a capability dashboard.
Action.imageAlt: Terminal screenshot of the llm-citeops overview capability dashboard

Compatibility.title: Compatibility
Compatibility.subtitle: Runs anywhere you run Node—local, CI, or container.

Reviews.title: What people say
Reviews.subtitle: Real quotes from users will appear here as they come in.
Reviews.emptyBody: We are collecting feedback from teams using CiteOps. Check back, or star the repo to follow releases.

Cta.title: Start auditing before the next release
Cta.subtitle: Install globally or run with npx. Reports are local—no account, no cloud dependency for the core CLI.

Footer.rights: CiteOps (llm-citeops) is MIT licensed. Respect robots.txt, terms of use, and rate limits when auditing third-party sites.
